front.title: JSContact Profile for RESTful Provisioning Protocol (RPP)

abstract.t: This document defines the JSContact Profile as defined in for the RESTful Provisioning Protocol (RPP). The JSContact Profile for RPP specifies how the JSContact format defined in can be used as a standardized representation of contact information within RPP operations, enabling interoperability and consistency across RPP implementations that manage contact data.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: TODO: write introduction, motivation, scope, etc.

section.name: Terminology
section.t: In this document the following terminology is used.
RESTful Provisioning Protocol or RPP - The protocol described in this document.
URL - A Uniform Resource Locator as defined in .
RPP client - An HTTP user agent performing an RPP request
RPP server - An HTTP server responsible for processing requests and returning results in any supported media type.

section.name: Conventions Used in This Document
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT","SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in .

section.name: JSContact Profile for RPP
section.t: Since JSContact is a generalized representation of contact data, many of its capabilities are not optimized for interoperable use in RPP payloads. This document therefore defines a usage profile for JSContact that simplifies implementation for both RPP clients and RPP servers.
The JSContact profile for RPP is compliant with the rules described in . The profile properties are formally listed in . All types mentioned in this section refer to the JSContact specification .

section.name: Kind
section.t: The Card "kind" member value MUST be "individual" (default) or "org" to represent an individual or an organization, respectively.

section.name: Language
section.t: The Card "language" member SHOULD be set when localizations are specified (i.e., when the "localizations" member is present and non-null).

section.name: Name
section.t: The Card "name" member MUST include the "full" member and MAY include the "components" member.
When present, each NameComponent MUST include only the "kind" and "value" members. The "kind" member value MUST be "title", "given" or "surname".

section.name: Organizations
section.t: The Organization type MUST include only the "org" member.

section.name: Addresses
section.t: The Address type MUST include at least one of the "full", "components", or "countryCode" members.
When present, each AddressComponent MUST include only the "kind" and "value" members. The "kind" member value MUST be "name", "locality", "region", "postcode", or "country".

section.name: Emails
section.t: The EmailAddress type MUST include only the "address" member.

section.name: Phones
section.t: The Phone type MUST include the "number" member and MAY include the "features" member. When the "features" member is present, its values MUST be "voice" or "fax". When the "features" member is absent, the phone number is assumed to be a voice number.

section.name: Links
section.t: The Link type MUST include the "uri" member and MAY include the "kind" member. When the "kind" member is present, its value MUST be "contact".

section.name: Map Keys
section.t: Since most JSContact collections are represented as maps, map keys must be defined. A JSContact map key MUST comply with the Id type definition in . To aid interoperability, RPP implementations SHOULD use the following predefined keys:

ul.li: "org" in the "organizations" map for a single preferred organization.
"addr" in the "addresses" map for a single preferred postal address.
"email" in the "emails" map for the preferred email address.
"voice" in the "phones" map for the preferred voice number.
"fax" in the "phones" map for the preferred fax number.
"url" in the "links" map for a preferred contact URL; the "kind" member of the Link object MUST NOT be set.
"contact-uri" in the "links" map for a contact URI; the "kind" member of the Link object MUST be set to "contact".

section.t: When internationalized and localized versions of an organization, postal address, or email exist, the internationalized version MUST be included under the predefined map key, while the localized version MUST be placed in the "localizations" map as described in .

section.name: Localizations
section.t: A RPP request MUST NOT include a "localizations" member, as RPP clients are not expected to provide localized contact information. However, A RPP response MAY include a "localizations" member to provide localized versions of the contact information.

If present, localized variants of name, organization, postal address, and email MUST be added to the "localizations" map. RPP implementations MUST expand all localizations, meaning a nested PatchObject key of the form "{key1}/{key2}/.../{keyN}" MUST NOT be used.
The following is an elided example of a Card including a localized version of the postal address:

figure.name: Example of handling localizations in JSContact

section.name: Profile Properties
section.t: The properties of the JSContact profile for RPP registered in the JSContact Profile registry as described in are:
| Property | Context | Restricted | Restricted Enum |
Client validation MUST return an error if any JSContact properties other than those listed above are present.

section.name: JSContact Profile Registry
section.t: IANA is requested to register the following entry in the "JSContact Profile" registry [I-D.ietf-calext-jscontact-profiles]:
Name: rpp Profile Version: 1.0 Reference: TODO
